subject: Product Management
title: Amazon Fire Phone
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Amazon Fire Phone.

hook.text: Amazon spent four years and hundreds of millions building the Fire Phone. It sold so poorly that Amazon wrote off $170 million.

buildup.text: The Fire Phone had a 3D display with 'Dynamic Perspective' — six front-facing cameras tracked your head movements.

discovery.text: The 3D feature was a gimmick nobody asked for. Customers wanted a great phone, not a parlor trick.

twist.text: The real purpose was Firefly — a feature that scanned objects to buy them on Amazon. It was a shopping tool disguised as a phone.

climax.text: Customers saw through it. They didn't want a phone built to sell them things. The Fire Phone was dead within a year.

punchline.text: Build for the user's needs, not your business model.

quiz.question: Why did the Amazon Fire Phone fail?
options: It prioritized gimmicks and Amazon's shopping goals over genuine user needs
It was too expensive for the market
